assumptions.objective: Compare ML trading strategies against buy-and-hold on the current oil next-day return test window.
assumptions.walk_forward_contract: Models are trained before validation/test in the staged ML run; trading thresholds are selected on 2022 validation and frozen for 2023+ test.
assumptions.reversal_cost_note: Position changes are charged by turnover; +1 to -1 costs 2 * transaction_cost.
assumptions.benchmark: Buy & Hold is long oil over the same target-date test window.

research_notes: Validation-only threshold selection reduces but does not eliminate backtest overfitting risk.
One-day execution lag prevents same-bar signal execution.
Cost sensitivity is shown because turnover can erase small classification edges.

models.label: XGBoost
models.description: XGB trading candidate selected from the staged XGB family by validation Sharpe, then converted to a signed expected-return score.

models.label: Random Forest
models.description: Baseline RandomForest classification artifact converted to a signed expected-return score using validation calibration.

models.label: Ridge
models.description: Ridge regression fit on train rows to predict next-day oil return directly.

models.label: Buy & Hold
models.description: Long oil exposure throughout the test period with one entry cost.

comparison.description: Long oil exposure throughout the test period with one entry cost.
